title: Goodbye, Missoula!
summary:
After months of scheming and building, we are officially living life on the road!

We don't really know when or how we decided to spend 2026 living in a van, but sometime in the past year, we both came to the conclusion that we were ready for a new adventure. What better way to spend the first year of our marriage than in a tiny space, together 24/7? We're about a month in and I think we made the right call.

In July of last year, Colin bought a one way ticket to Vancouver, WA where he picked up a partially built 2021 Ram Promaster and drove it back to Missoula. When I first stepped inside the van, I thought it was both the biggest car and the tiniest home I had ever been in.

The van came with a platform bed, solar installed and insulation hanging out of the holes. Neither of us had real experience with woodworking, electrical, plumbing, etc, but after many hours of YouTube University, we came up with a design and set forth. We had about two months after our wedding to complete the build enough for us to move ourselves into the van. We spent many hours in the garage until 9pm working with our headlamps. Since we were beginners at everything, we spent a lot of time cutting and recutting, drilling and redrilling.

We finished the build with just days to spare, sold both of our cars, rented out our house in Missoula and officially moved into the van at the end of October. Our plan is to take the whole year off of work, though both of our jobs are giving us the option to come back next year if we want which we are very grateful for.

With our days no longer filled with work, we have gotten to bike, climb, run, and read a lot. Follow along here to see where the road takes us! You can click on the map tab to view an interactive map of our travels. Also, sign up to our mailing list by filling out the form at the bottom of the screen! If you encounter any bugs on this site or have any other issues, send Colin an email at colin.brust@gmail.com

-Helen & Colin
